Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Arterial blood

Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay
aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine, mild hyperkalemia), marked hyperbilirubinemia, mild hyperchloremia, metabolic acidosis (low pH, low O2 saturation), and dyslipidemia (high total and LDL cholesterol). Liver enzymes are normal. Hematology and coagulation are within normal limits. Findings suggest acute or chronic kidney injury with possible secondary metabolic and respiratory disturbances; urgent clinical evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
